name: Audit Log
description: Append-only JSONL audit log for recording and reading operation history. Provides functions to log commands with arguments, results, and errors, and to retrieve recent audit entries.

types.name: AuditEntry
types.description: Structure of an audit log entry (not explicitly defined but implied by usage)

functions.name: _audit_path
functions.description: Returns the Path object for the audit log file (audit.jsonl in HISTORY_DIR).
preconditions: HISTORY_DIR must be defined in ascend.config
postconditions: Returns Path object pointing to HISTORY_DIR/audit.jsonl

functions.name: log_operation
functions.description: Append an operation to the audit log. Creates parent directories if they don't exist. Writes a JSON line with timestamp, command, and optional args, result, and error fields.

error_cases.name: file_write_error
error_cases.condition: Unable to write to audit file due to permissions or disk space

error_cases.name: json_serialization_error
error_cases.condition: Arguments contain non-serializable objects (mitigated by default=str)

preconditions: command must be a valid string
postconditions: Parent directory of audit log exists
A new JSON line is appended to the audit log file
Entry contains ISO8601 UTC timestamp and command
Entry contains args, result, error only if provided and truthy

structured_side_effects.description: Appends JSON line to audit log

functions.name: read_audit
functions.description: Read recent audit entries from the audit log. Returns the last N entries (default 50). Returns empty list if audit file does not exist.

inputs.name: last_n
inputs.description: Number of most recent entries to return

error_cases.name: file_read_error
error_cases.condition: Unable to read audit file due to permissions

error_cases.name: json_decode_error
error_cases.condition: Malformed JSON line in audit file

postconditions: Returns empty list if audit file doesn't exist
Entries are returned in chronological order (oldest to newest of the last_n)
Empty lines in the file are skipped

invariants: Audit log is append-only (never modified or deleted by this module)
Each audit entry is a single JSON line (JSONL format)
All timestamps are in UTC and ISO8601 format
Audit file path is always HISTORY_DIR/audit.jsonl

data_access.rationale: This module writes audit logs to the filesystem and reads them back for reporting

side_effects.rationale: Append-only audit trail for all operations
Read audit history for reporting and analysis
Creates parent directories if they don't exist

authority.rationale: This module is the authoritative source for audit log operations. It owns the format, location, and access patterns for the audit trail.
